title: The Changing Of The Guardian

jokes.text: D'oh.
jokes.setup: Opening moment establishing Homer's presence
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer catchphrase as opening
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase that's perfectly integrated but predictable as an opener

jokes.text: Even the kids on the box look bored. They're miserable.
jokes.setup: Family complaining about boring board game
jokes.punchline: Visual observation about the game box showing unhappy children
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor that works on multiple levels - visual gag plus meta-commentary

jokes.text: No! Whenever we play that, my elbow touches Dad's junk.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting Twister as alternative game
jokes.punchline: Bart's specific anatomical complaint about family game
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with specific physical detail that feels earned

jokes.text: if thy tornado must take me, please let it take me to Oz. But don't let Flanders be the scarecrow.
jokes.setup: Marge praying during tornado at Flanders' house
jokes.punchline: Wizard of Oz reference with specific Flanders dig
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference with character-specific neighbor antagonism

jokes.text: This counts as a walk!
jokes.setup: Bart being carried by tornado winds
jokes.punchline: Treating dangerous situation like exercise routine
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic from Bart finding silver lining in chaos

jokes.text: What? 'Cause they rented a van and taped an antenna to the top? Not just any tape. Duct tape.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning storm chasers' professionalism
jokes.punchline: Carl's deadpan defense of duct tape as professional equipment
jokes.explanation: Well-structured setup/punchline with good deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Don't worry, Marge, we bought a ton of safety gear from a highly-respected storm chaser's widow. It's not what you're thinking. He died of a heart attack. Yeah, 80 feet up in the air.
jokes.setup: Reassuring Marge about safety equipment source
jokes.punchline: Escalating revelation about how the storm chaser died
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with dark comedy payoff

jokes.text: I'd say she's prettier than a surface hoar. Hey! Surely, you didn't misunderstand my usage of 'surface hoar.' The sublimation of ice crystals that's colder than a frost point.
jokes.setup: Carl trying to compliment Marge with weather terminology
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding over scientific term that sounds inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay joke with scientific explanation as punchline

jokes.text: What women really want is a guy who's confident enough to go completely downhill after marriage and know she'll still love him.
jokes.setup: Discussion about what women want
jokes.punchline: Homer's cynical but self-aware take on marriage
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy that reveals Homer's self-awareness while being universally relatable

jokes.text: It's like God's vacuum cleaner.
jokes.setup: Homer observing tornado
jokes.punchline: Simple domestic metaphor for natural disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer-esque simple metaphor with great economy

jokes.text: Help me, God. What is it I'm paying you for every Sunday?
jokes.setup: Homer in distress from tornado
jokes.punchline: Treating religion like a paid service
jokes.explanation: Good character-based comedy about Homer's transactional view of religion

jokes.text: Can... always use... a good babysitter. I was just 27 years from retirement!
jokes.setup: Police officer trapped and dying
jokes.punchline: Treating death like early retirement
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy with good structure treating death as bureaucratic inconvenience

jokes.text: He was a black guy, but his voice sounded like a white guy. Hey, excuse me for wanting to fit in.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to remember Carl
jokes.punchline: Carl's response about his voice acting choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about voice acting that breaks fourth wall cleverly

jokes.text: Once again, the big banks stick it to the little guy.
jokes.setup: Family trapped in bank by debris
jokes.punchline: Homer blaming banks for physical entrapment
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke that works on literal and metaphorical levels

jokes.text: Police have arrived on the scene after having fled the state at the first sign of the storm.
jokes.setup: News report about police response
jokes.punchline: Police fled then returned for photo op
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of media coverage and police response to disasters

jokes.text: What is this, a bird? It's a bird known as a crane. And it got very sick on the way over. Well, our top priority is making sure that bird gets well.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum misunderstanding crane equipment
jokes.punchline: Treating construction crane as actual bird needing medical care
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum absurdist logic with escalating commitment to the misunderstanding

jokes.text: Uh, want to go halfsies on a pizza? FYI, that means you get one slice.
jokes.setup: Casual offer to share pizza
jokes.punchline: Revealing extremely unfair split
jokes.explanation: Solid setup/punchline with good deadpan delivery of unfair terms

jokes.text: Were you worried about me, boy? Sure. Why not? Clearly, the boy's in shock. Oh, yeah, I'm a wreck.
jokes.setup: Homer seeking emotional validation from Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's completely disinterested responses
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Bart's emotional detachment and Homer's delusion

jokes.text: Connecticut? No, our state.
jokes.setup: Marge mentioning kids could become wards of the state
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about which state
jokes.explanation: Simple misunderstanding joke that works for Homer's character

jokes.text: Oh, you'd let me have another chance after the way I screwed things up with you? Eh, good point. We'll find somebody else.
jokes.setup: Grandpa offering to raise the kids
jokes.punchline: Immediately agreeing that he's unqualified
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment with Grandpa's honesty about his parenting failures

jokes.text: Hi. You've reach Herb Powell. I'm poor again.
jokes.setup: Calling Homer's rich brother for help
jokes.punchline: Answering machine revealing he's poor again
jokes.explanation: Good callback with perfect economy - entire character arc in four words

jokes.text: That's some impressive flute-nastics. Shut up! You can't praise her. She'll think she's smart and slack off. Then all our loving would be for nothing.
jokes.setup: Homer complimenting Ling's musical gymnastics
jokes.punchline: Patty's extreme tiger parenting philosophy
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay plus character-specific parenting satire

jokes.text: Hey, Luanne, you always asked me what I saw in Vicky at the Walgreens. Well, I'll tell you what I saw: not you!
jokes.setup: Kirk trying to defend his marriage commitment
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing affair in worst possible way
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy with perfect character-specific foot-in-mouth moment

jokes.text: Anything new that wanders into this house winds up in the stew pot.
jokes.setup: Cletus family considering taking three more kids
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about what happens to new arrivals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus dark humor with excellent character integration

jokes.text: Give me back my pants. I've got to get back to my unit.
jokes.setup: Couple discussing adoption
jokes.punchline: Military man demanding pants back
jokes.explanation: Random absurd moment, lacks context but has economy

jokes.text: Wait a minute, I live here. Aw.
jokes.setup: Homer thinking coast is clear to return home
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's already at his own house
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confusion moment with perfect economy

jokes.text: if we should fail to die together as a family.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining guardianship to kids
jokes.punchline: Framing survival as failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect Homer logic inversion

jokes.text: you guys are trying to find a couple of saps to take us when Dad has a coronary in the dead of night, rolls over and crushes Mom.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining what guardianship really means
jokes.punchline: Vivid specific scenario of Homer's death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with Lisa's intelligence applied to morbid scenario

jokes.text: That's where they filmed The Wire! Step on it!
jokes.setup: Homer hearing about Mid-Atlantic accent
jokes.punchline: Confusing geography with TV show, then fleeing in panic
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's pop culture confusion and cowardice

jokes.text: That's Moe and a bag of garbage. Boy, cats sure breed a lot.
jokes.setup: Looking for potential guardian couples
jokes.punchline: Mistaking Moe with trash for romantic couple, then non-sequitur about cats
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag plus classic Homer random observation chain

jokes.text: Wow, a man's stomach can be flat?
jokes.setup: Homer seeing fit surfer Mav
jokes.punchline: Genuine amazement at basic fitness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer body awareness comedy with great character integration

jokes.text: And in my free time, I prosecute record companies who withhold royalties from elderly blues musicians.
jokes.setup: Portia being impressive as environmental lawyer
jokes.punchline: Even more impressive pro bono work
jokes.explanation: Character establishing perfection through good deeds escalation

jokes.text: I love two things: shutting up and giving away money.
jokes.setup: Introduction to Mav's father
jokes.punchline: Perfect grandfather qualities stated bluntly
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure - perfect summary of ideal grandfather in six words

jokes.text: No, I turned it down because Cecil Rhodes was such a racist.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Portia's impressive credentials
jokes.punchline: Turning down prestigious scholarship for moral reasons
jokes.explanation: Good character building showing Portia's moral superiority complex

jokes.text: Uh, say, listen, Portia. How would you and Mav feel about... um, swinging? Uh, with you guys? Uh, seems like my husband would be getting the much better deal.
jokes.setup: Homer awkwardly approaching topic of guardianship
jokes.punchline: Sexual misunderstanding with Portia's cutting response
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with misdirection and sharp comeback

jokes.text: All you need to know is I'm a politeness monster who eats please and farts thank-yous, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Portia saying it's hard to know a child after one day
jokes.punchline: Bart's ridiculous self-description using digestive metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist comedy with great quotable phrasing

jokes.text: It's empty. You never know when you might want to mail something.
jokes.setup: Homer giving envelope to help their decision
jokes.punchline: Empty envelope with Homer's nonsensical reasoning
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic with absurd gift and reasoning

jokes.text: It's empty. You never know when you might want to mail something.
jokes.setup: Giving envelope as gift to impress them
jokes.punchline: Empty envelope as practical gift
jokes.explanation: Homer's illogical generosity presented as practical

jokes.text: You don't look a Trojan horse in the mouth.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing concern about moving too fast
jokes.punchline: Homer mixing idioms about gift horses and Trojan horses
jokes.explanation: Good Homer malapropism mixing two different sayings

jokes.text: I'm going to decrease the volume of my voice and mumble so you can't really understand what I'm saying, and I'll just keep doing it until the subject...
jokes.setup: Mav explaining where Bart is
jokes.punchline: Describing exactly what he's doing while doing it
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about dialogue techniques in TV

jokes.text: Suckers! Oh, wait, you've got to initial this. Checkmate.
jokes.setup: Homer thinking he's outsmarted them
jokes.punchline: Realizing he still needs their cooperation
jokes.explanation: Homer's premature celebration deflated by bureaucracy

jokes.text: The one thing people with kids desperately need is no kids.
jokes.setup: Homer enjoying couples time without children
jokes.punchline: Blunt truth about parenting
jokes.explanation: Honest parenting observation with excellent economy

jokes.text: Marge, no indulgence of mine ever becomes a habit.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about making weekend visits a habit
jokes.punchline: Homer's claim about his self-control
jokes.explanation: Ironic given Homer's many addictions and habits

jokes.text: The one thing people with kids desperately need is no kids.
jokes.setup: Enjoying couples time
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of parenting
jokes.explanation: Relatable parenting observation with good economy

jokes.text: I don't like the way they just rip the paper off. Santa's Little Helper, my ass.
jokes.setup: Complaining about pets and Christmas presents
jokes.punchline: Dog's name doesn't match helpful behavior
jokes.explanation: Good observation about pet behavior vs their names

jokes.text: No one steals my kids, except me, from that theme park jail that I never told you about, except I guess I just did.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring no one steals his kids
jokes.punchline: Revealing his own past kidnapping and accidentally confessing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Homer self-incrimination

jokes.text: Like all childless couples, they troll the beaches waiting for the egg-layers to wander off from their young.
jokes.setup: Homer theorizing about Mav and Portia's motives
jokes.punchline: Treating humans like sea turtles with predatory childless couples
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist metaphor comparing humans to sea creatures with perfect Homer paranoia

jokes.text: those kids are ours, because we made them by accident.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why kids belong to them despite better guardians
jokes.punchline: Honest admission about accidental conception
jokes.explanation: Good honest parenting humor with Homer's blunt logic

jokes.text: I'm a lawyer; he's a surfer. That combination's pretty unstoppable.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning how they could get the kids
jokes.punchline: Portia's confidence in their professional/lifestyle combination
jokes.explanation: Decent character comedy showing Portia's overconfidence in their lifestyle

jokes.text: you're leaving a gap in our lives that can only be filled by foreign travel, sleeping late and gourmet food.
jokes.setup: Portia sad about losing the children
jokes.punchline: Listing luxurious consolation prizes
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing their privileged lifestyle perspective

jokes.text: If you call this living. Why, you little... I do call this living. Living the way kids in India can only dream of.
jokes.setup: Homer saying they'll concentrate on living
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcasm leading to Homer's inappropriate global comparison
jokes.explanation: Good father-son dynamic with Homer's tone-deaf global perspective

jokes.text: I win. Win what? I forget.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring victory
jokes.punchline: Immediately forgetting what he was competing for
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment with excellent economy showing his simple satisfaction and memory issues

tmdb_overview: A tornado touches down in Springfield which sucks up Santa's Little Helper. While Homer and Marge are out looking for the family dog, the tornado strikes again and almost takes Marge and Homer. Marge is distraught about the kids and decide to pick a  guardian to care for the children.
